notes:
Ừm = “um” in English. It shows you’re thinking, hesitating, or not 100% sure.

It’s a filler sound, usually at the beginning of a sentence while you buy time to think. It can also soften what you say, so you don’t sound too direct.

Very similar to ờ (also “uh/um”). Slightly softer and more thoughtful than a quick “à” (which is more like “oh”).
notes_md:
**Ừm** = “um” in English. It shows you’re thinking, hesitating, or not 100% sure.

It’s a filler sound, usually at the beginning of a sentence while you buy time to think. It can also soften what you say, so you don’t sound too direct.


Very similar to **ờ** (also “uh/um”). Slightly softer and more thoughtful than a quick “à” (which is more like “oh”).

definitions.es: Un sonido utilizado para expresar duda o para indicar que se está pensando.
definitions.vi: Âm thanh được sử dụng để diễn tả sự do dự hoặc để chỉ ra rằng đang suy nghĩ.
definitions.en: A sound used to express hesitation or to indicate thinking.

exampleSentencesTranslated: Um, I think we should go to the movies tonight.
Um, I am not sure about this answer.
Um, can you help me with my math homework?
exampleSentences: Ừm, anh nghĩ chúng ta nên đi xem phim tối nay.
Ừm, em chưa chắc về câu trả lời này đâu.
Ừm, chị có thể giúp em làm bài tập toán không?
